ncs_standard_definition.description_short: A nematologist is a scientist who studies nematodes, microscopic worms that can harm crops and plants. They develop strategies to control and manage nematode populations.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Nematologists work in agricultural research institutions, universities, and government organizations. They conduct experiments, collect and analyze data, and develop new technologies to control nematodes. They may also collaborate with farmers, extension workers, and other stakeholders to implement nematode management practices.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: M.Sc. in Agriculture or related field

first_stepper.description: Pursue a degree in agriculture or a related field, focusing on plant pathology or entomology.

choice_maker.description: Bridge the gap by acquiring relevant certifications or courses in nematology.

future_builder.description: Gain experience in agricultural research or extension services, and develop advanced skills in nematode management.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2-3 years as Junior Research Fellow or Extension Officer

problem_solver_uturn.description: Switching from an unrelated field, focus on developing survival skills and building a portfolio.
